organizations.role: Founder and designer of the Washington, DC-based bespoke luxury lighting and furniture line, launched 2021; fixtures bench-made in Italy, retailed through the Liz O'Brien Gallery in New York from Fall 2022
organizations.notes: This is almost certainly Tyler Hill, the designer behind Transparent — and critically for this record, he is an Austin, Texas native, which is what explains a Washington, DC donor giving to Kirk Watson. Four details in the filing corroborate independently: he is repeatedly described as 'based in Washington D.C.' (20037 is the West End/Foggy Bottom zip); the occupation 'Designer' matches; the unusual one-word employer 'Transparent' matches his brand exactly — Charleston City Paper reported in April 2021 that his debut line, 'aptly named Transparent,' had debuted to designers before expanding to markets including Washington and Texas, and the collection is now sold through the Liz O'Brien Gallery in New York and at transparentatelier.com; and that same profile identifies him as 'a 39-year-old Austin, Texas native' who came to Charleston in 2010 to study design at the Art Institute of Charleston, putting his birth around 1981-82. His work is bespoke lighting and furniture in black and bronze metals, glass, alabaster and leather, drawing on brutalist architecture; before Transparent he was a partner in Mitchell Hill, the Hasell Street design center and gallery in Charleston. Classified Architecture per the standing convention in this dataset, which places interior and furnishing designers (Dawn Thompson Interiors, Kristen Nix Interiors, Debra Hurt Interior Design) under that label rather than Retail or Self-Employed. Held at medium rather than high for one reason: every published source calls him simply 'Tyler Hill,' and no source confirms 'Richard' as his legal first name, so the first name in this filing is unverified — though using a middle name professionally is unremarkable, and no competing Richard/Tyler Hill designer in DC surfaced. The fec_donation_count of 0 is accurate rather than a matching failure: an FEC search for employer 'Transparent' returns 1,734 records, none of them a Hill in DC, and a search for contributors named Hill in Washington, DC with occupation 'Designer' returns 122 records belonging entirely to Johnny Hill, a graphic designer at Marcom Group in 20003 making $3-$25 ActBlue-style gifts — a different person with a different zip, employer and giving profile. No AIPAC, pro-Israel, oil & gas, gun or defense affiliation surfaced.
